name: Leptoquark explanation of B-anomalies
summary: Tree-level exchange of a TeV-scale leptoquark (vector U1 in (3, 1, 2/3) of SU(3)xSU(2)xU(1), or scalar S1 + R2) couples preferentially to third-generation fermions and produces a positive shift in R(D*) consistent with the HFLAV measurement. Vector U1 from a Pati-Salam embedding is the canonical 'minimal' fit; scalar combinations also work. Provides a UV-grounded interpretation of the B-anomaly cluster.

addresses_puzzles.note: TeV-scale U1 leptoquark with Wilson coefficient C_LL ~ 0.1 at the LQ scale reproduces R(D*) ~ 0.290 (vs 0.254 SM); consistent with R(K), B_s -> mu mu, B -> K^* mu mu at SM levels. LHC direct searches for pair-produced leptoquarks have closed below ~1.5 TeV; resonant searches in pp -> tau tau bound around 4-5 TeV.

touches_benchmarks.note: Predicted positive shift in R(D*) matches HFLAV 3.2 sigma anomaly.
Pure third-generation LQ doesn't disturb b -> s ll neutral-current observables (where R(K) sits at SM).
If leptoquark couples to all three lepton generations, mu -> e gamma loops constrain the off-diagonal Yukawa structure tightly.

new_fields: vector leptoquark U1 (3, 1, 2/3) -- from Pati-Salam SU(4)
new_symmetries: implicit gauge or flavor-protection structure to evade FCNC bounds
introduces.note: Leptoquark mass and flavor-structured Yukawa couplings.

history: Bauer-Neubert (2015, arXiv:1511.01900) early scalar LQ fit. Buttazzo-Greljo-Isidori-Marzocca (2017, arXiv:1706.07808), 'B-physics anomalies: a guide to combined explanations', JHEP 11, 044 — minimal U1 fit. Active phenomenological / LHC programme.

references.citation: Buttazzo, Greljo, Isidori, Marzocca, 'B-physics anomalies: a guide to combined explanations', JHEP 11, 044 (2017)
